User Story: As a developer I want a CSS-only button with variants and sizes so that I can build action triggers without JavaScript.
Expected Behaviour: `.btn` is an inline-flex element with primary bg/fg, 2.5rem height, focus-visible ring; variants override bg/color; sizes adjust height/padding/font.
Edge Cases: btn-link sets height:auto and padding:0, breaking visual alignment in btn-group; btn-icon combined with btn-sm/btn-lg resizes to square; hover suppressed when disabled or aria-busy
Notes: Rules: hover/active states gated by :not(:disabled,[aria-busy]) | focus ring uses --ring token || Deps: base/tokens (--primary,--secondary,--ring,--radius-*,--space-*) || Assumes: Author applies semantic <button> element

User Story: As a developer I want a loading spinner with no JS so that aria-busy buttons show progress automatically.
Expected Behaviour: `.btn[aria-busy=true]::after` injects a 1em rotating ring via shadcss-spin keyframes.
Edge Cases: Spinner appended after text content, not replacing it — both show; btn-link has padding:0 so spinner may crowd text
Notes: Rules: spinner uses currentColor border with transparent right edge || Deps: base/tokens (--radius-full) | CSS @keyframes support || Assumes: Author sets aria-busy=true attribute manually

User Story: As a developer I want inline status badges with semantic color variants so that I can label content states.
Expected Behaviour: `.badge` is inline-flex pill; variants set bg/fg from tokens; `.badge-dot::before` prepends a 0.375rem currentColor dot.
Edge Cases: badge-dot dot color follows currentColor, so on badge-outline the dot uses foreground; success/warning/info variants depend on non-shadcn tokens that must exist
Notes: Rules: dot uses currentColor and radius-full || Deps: base/tokens (--success/--warning/--info and -foreground) || Assumes: Badges are non-interactive spans

User Story: As a developer I want an aria-pressed toggle button so that I get accessible two-state controls with no JS for styling.
Expected Behaviour: `.toggle` is a 2.5rem square button; [aria-pressed=true] applies accent bg/fg; sizes and outline variant defined.
Edge Cases: .has-text widens to auto with horizontal padding; aria-pressed toggling itself requires JS — CSS only styles the state; outline variant changes border-color when pressed
Notes: Rules: pressed state keyed on [aria-pressed=true] | svg children sized via direct child selector || Deps: base/tokens (--accent,--ring,--input) | JS or native to flip aria-pressed (not provided) || Assumes: Author uses <button> with aria-pressed and toggles it externally

User Story: As a developer I want a single/multi-select toggle group built on native inputs so that selection state and form submission work without JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.toggle-group` wraps hidden radio/checkbox inputs each followed by a `.toggle-group-item` label; :checked + .toggle-group-item highlights the selected label. Outline/size/vertical variants via data-attributes.
Edge Cases: Adjacent-sibling selector requires input immediately before its label; outline first/last child only zero one side corners; middle items keep full radius; vertical orientation has no outline border-collapse handling
Test Cases: BTN-005-T1:happy:PASS; BTN-005-T2:accessibility:PASS; BTN-005-T3:invalid:FAIL; BTN-005-T4:boundary:FAIL; BTN-005-T5:responsive:PASS; BTN-005-T6:boundary:FAIL
Notes: Rules: inputs visually hidden but focusable proxy via :focus-visible + label ring | checked highlight uses background+shadow || Deps: base/tokens (--muted,--background,--ring,--shadow-sm) | native radio/checkbox semantics || Assumes: Markup orders <input> then sibling <label class=toggle-group-item>

User Story: As a tooling consumer I want registry.json to validate against its declared schema so that automated installers can parse component metadata.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares shadcn $schema but uses a custom shape: top-level components[] with keys name/file/deps/markup/classes — not shadcn registry item schema.
Edge Cases: shadcn schema expects fields like type/files[].path/registryDependencies; this uses file/deps; schema URL implies validation that would fail
Notes: Rules: Declared $schema = https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json || Deps: shadcn schema (cited, not conformed) || Assumes: Consumers may attempt to validate against the cited schema

Feature Name: Text input base, states, sizes, file & icon variants
User Story: As a form author I want a styled single-line input with focus/hover/disabled/invalid states, size modifiers, file styling, and icon wrapping so that inputs match the design system with zero JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.input` is a 2.5rem full-width bordered field with token-driven colors and a focus ring; disabled dims to 0.5; invalid border turns destructive only when a value is present; .input-sm/.input-lg adjust height/padding/font; file inputs get styled file-selector-button; .input-wrap positions an absolute icon and pads the input.
Edge Cases: Input with no placeholder never matches :not(:placeholder-shown) so :invalid styling never applies; Trailing-only icon: input still gets 2.5rem leading padding; RTL handled via inset-inline / padding-inline logical props
Test Cases: FRM-001-T1:happy:PASS; FRM-001-T2:boundary:FAIL; FRM-001-T3:happy:PASS; FRM-001-T4:error:FAIL; FRM-001-T5:accessibility:PASS; FRM-001-T6:responsive:PASS
Notes: Rules: Invalid border requires both :invalid and a non-empty value via :placeholder-shown | Hover border only when not disabled and not focused || Deps: base/tokens | Browser support for ::file-selector-button and inset-inline-* logical props || Assumes: A placeholder attribute is present on inputs that want invalid styling | Icons are direct svg children of .input-wrap

User Story: As a form author I want a multi-line textarea with the same visual language as inputs so forms are consistent.
Expected Behaviour: `.textarea` is a block 100%-width field, min-height 5rem, vertical resize, with hover/focus/disabled/invalid states mirroring .input.
Edge Cases: Empty placeholder-less textarea never shows invalid border; resize:vertical only; No size modifiers provided unlike input
Notes: Rules: Invalid border requires :invalid AND a value via :placeholder-shown || Deps: base/tokens (--input,--ring,--destructive,--space-*) || Assumes: Authors add a placeholder when they want invalid styling

Feature Name: Label, required marker, and legacy form-field layout
User Story: As a form author I want labels that dim with disabled controls, show a required asterisk, and a simple stacked form-field helper.
Expected Behaviour: `.label` is an inline-flex 500-weight clickable label; dims via :has(:disabled,[aria-disabled]); .label-required::after adds a destructive asterisk; .form-field stacks children with gap and turns help-text destructive when a contained control is invalid-with-value.
Edge Cases: :has() unsupported browsers lose disabled-dim and invalid help-text; help-text destructive again gated on :placeholder-shown; cursor:pointer on label even when not associated with a control
Notes: Rules: Disabled dim requires a descendant :disabled or aria-disabled | Help-text error requires :invalid:not(:placeholder-shown) descendant || Deps: base/tokens | CSS :has() selector support || Assumes: Label wraps or is sibling-grouped with its control inside .form-field

Feature Name: High-level field wrapper with grid areas and :has() validation
User Story: As a form author I want a field that lays out label/control/description/error in a grid and reveals the error when the control is invalid, with required asterisk via data-required.
Expected Behaviour: `.field` is a grid with label/control/description/error areas; asterisk shown only when [data-required=true]; .field-error hidden by default and shown via :has(:invalid:not(:placeholder-shown)) or :has(select:invalid), hiding description and applying destructive borders.
Edge Cases: Required field without placeholder: error never shows for text inputs; Comment claims :user-invalid lacks browser support — now widely supported; Multiple invalid controls in one field all trigger via :has; Errors show before blur (no :user-invalid debounce)
Test Cases: FRM-004-T1:happy:PASS; FRM-004-T2:boundary:FAIL; FRM-004-T3:happy:PASS; FRM-004-T4:error:FAIL; FRM-004-T5:accessibility:FAIL; FRM-004-T6:performance:PASS
Notes: Rules: Text/textarea error reveal requires :invalid AND non-empty | Select error reveal requires only select:invalid || Deps: base/tokens | label component | CSS :has() | input/textarea/select components || Assumes: Inputs carry a placeholder so :placeholder-shown can detect empty state | field-error markup is always present as a .field child

User Story: As a consumer using a shadcn-compatible tool I want registry.json to accurately describe these components against its declared schema.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares $schema https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json and lists input/textarea/label/field with file/deps/markup/classes.
Edge Cases: Top-level shape differs from shadcn registry schema; input deps omit no entry; field deps cite label and base/tokens
Notes: Rules: Entries should validate against the cited $schema || Deps: shadcn registry JSON schema || Assumes: Consumers may attempt schema validation

Feature Name: Native select with CSS chevron and sizes
User Story: As a developer I want a restyled native <select> so that I get shadcn appearance with built-in keyboard/screen-reader/form support and no JS or image assets.
Expected Behaviour: `.select` restyles a native <select> with appearance:none, an inline data-URI SVG chevron, focus ring, disabled dimming, and -sm/-lg size variants.
Edge Cases: Long option text overlapping the chevron area; RTL: background-position uses logical right; appearance:none does not restyle the native option dropdown popup
Notes: Rules: height fixed 2.5rem default, 2rem sm, 2.75rem lg | focus removes outline but adds 2px ring box-shadow | hover border only when :not(:disabled) || Deps: base/tokens | registry deps: base/tokens || Assumes: Token variables defined in base/tokens.css | Browser supports data-URI SVG backgrounds

User Story: As a developer I want a native checkbox styled to shadcn with a CSS checkmark and indeterminate visual so that forms and a11y work without JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.checkbox` restyles native input[type=checkbox] with clip-path checkmark on :checked and a dash glyph on [data-indeterminate=true].
Edge Cases: data-indeterminate is a static attribute, not the real IDL indeterminate property; Both :checked and data-indeterminate: checked ::before wins by source order; ::before checkmark color uses --primary-foreground
Notes: Rules: checked fills with --primary and scales checkmark to 1 | indeterminate uses a bar clip-path | focus-visible outline 2px ring | disabled opacity 0.5 || Deps: base/tokens (--border,--primary,--primary-foreground,--radius-sm,--ring) || Assumes: data-indeterminate set declaratively in HTML

User Story: As a developer I want native radios styled to shadcn with a CSS dot so that single-select groups work with native semantics.
Expected Behaviour: `.radio` styles a native radio with a scaled ::before dot on :checked; radio.css ALSO defines .radio-group as flex with vertical data-orientation override.
Edge Cases: radio.css and radio-group.css both define .radio-group with conflicting display; radio.css handles vertical; radio-group.css handles horizontal; Showcase radios in radio-group omit the .radio class
Notes: Rules: checked fills --primary, dot scale(1) | radius-full for circular shape | focus-visible 2px ring || Deps: base/tokens | registry deps: base/tokens || Assumes: Both radio.css and radio-group.css concatenate, radio-group.css after

User Story: As a developer I want a card-style radio group with labels/descriptions that highlights the selected option using :has() so it works with zero JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.radio-group` is a grid of .radio-group-item cards; :has(:checked) highlights selected and :has(:focus-visible) outlines focus; an inline variant strips card chrome.
Edge Cases: :has() unsupported in older browsers; data-variant=inline reflows to column grid auto-flow; item:has(:checked) matches ANY checked descendant input
Test Cases: SEL-004-T1:happy:PASS; SEL-004-T2:accessibility:PASS; SEL-004-T3:error:FAIL; SEL-004-T4:boundary:PASS; SEL-004-T5:responsive:PASS; SEL-004-T6:invalid:PASS
Notes: Rules: selected card: primary border + accent bg | inline variant removes padding/border/bg | label/description typography from tokens || Deps: base/tokens | radio | :has() selector support | native input[type=radio] grouping || Assumes: Each .radio-group-item wraps exactly one radio input | Browser supports :has()

User Story: As a user I want a toggle switch so that I can flip a boolean setting with native keyboard and screen-reader support.
Expected Behaviour: A native input type=checkbox with class .switch renders as a pill; :checked turns the track to --primary and slides the ::before knob; RTL flips knob direction; sm/lg size variants.
Edge Cases: RTL languages; disabled state opacity 0.5; focus-visible ring; sm/lg variants
Notes: Rules: knob translate distance must equal width - knob - 4px gap | checked uses --primary, unchecked uses --input || Deps: base/tokens | native checkbox semantics || Assumes: Author applies .switch to an input type=checkbox

User Story: As a user I want a styled range slider so that I can pick a numeric value with a visible filled portion.
Expected Behaviour: input[type=range].slider draws track/thumb cross-browser. Filled portion faked via a linear-gradient driven by --slider-fill (default 50%). Vertical via data-orientation; sm/lg size variants.
Edge Cases: 0% and 100% fill; vertical orientation; Firefox vs WebKit pseudo-elements; disabled
Test Cases: CTL-002-T1:happy:PASS; CTL-002-T2:error:FAIL; CTL-002-T3:boundary:PASS; CTL-002-T4:accessibility:PASS; CTL-002-T5:responsive:FAIL; CTL-002-T6:security:PASS
Notes: Rules: --slider-fill must be updated as value changes | gradient hard stops at --slider-fill || Deps: base/tokens | inline JS to update --slider-fill on input || Assumes: Author wires oninput to set --slider-fill

User Story: As a user I want segmented OTP input so that I can enter a one-time code digit by digit.
Expected Behaviour: CSS styles .otp container and .otp-input as individual mono boxes with focus ring, :disabled, and :invalid destructive border. .otp-separator styles a divider. sm/lg variants resize.
Edge Cases: empty value; invalid non-digit; auto-advance between boxes; paste full code
Notes: Rules: pattern=[0-9] + maxlength=1 per box | focus shows ring; invalid shows destructive border || Deps: base/tokens | JS for auto-advance/paste (absent) || Assumes: Each digit is a separate <input>

User Story: As a user I want a progress bar so that I can see determinate or indeterminate loading status.
Expected Behaviour: <progress class=progress> restyled cross-browser. With no [value] it matches :indeterminate and runs an animated gradient. Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion. Value transitions width with --duration-slow.
Edge Cases: value=0 and value=100; indeterminate (no value attr); reduced motion; Firefox vs WebKit pseudo-elements
Notes: Rules: determinate uses value/max | indeterminate animates only when no value || Deps: base/tokens | native <progress> semantics || Assumes: Author uses <progress> element

Feature Name: Registry schema conformance for these components
User Story: As a consumer of the registry I want entries that match the declared $schema so that tooling can install components.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares shadcn $schema and lists entries for input-otp, progress, slider, switch with fields name/file/deps/description/markup/classes.
Edge Cases: shadcn schema expects items[] with files[], type, registryDependencies; custom fields deps/classes/markup not in shadcn schema
Notes: Rules: entries must conform to cited $schema || Deps: https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json || Assumes: The cited schema is shadcn registry schema

User Story: As a developer I want a composable card with header/content/footer zones so that I can group related content in a bordered surface.
Expected Behaviour: `.card` is a column flexbox with card bg/fg, 1px border, --radius-lg, --shadow-sm and overflow:hidden. Header/content/footer pad --space-6; content/footer drop top padding.
Edge Cases: card-content followed by card-footer reduces bottom padding via :has(); footer with no data-justify defaults to flex-start
Notes: Rules: card-content uses :has(+ .card-footer) requiring :has() | data-justify only honors end|between || Deps: base/tokens | base/theme | CSS :has() || Assumes: Tokens --card,--card-foreground,--border,--radius-lg,--shadow-sm defined

User Story: As a developer I want an opt-in hover lift on clickable cards so that interactivity is signaled.
Expected Behaviour: .card-interactive transitions transform+box-shadow and on hover translates -2px and raises to --shadow-md.
Edge Cases: Hover-only effect; no focus or keyboard state; Touch devices have no hover
Notes: Rules: Effect is opt-in via separate class || Deps: base/tokens (--duration-base,--ease-out,--shadow-md) || Assumes: Card used as a non-focusable div in showcase

User Story: As a developer I want an inline alert with icon/title/description and semantic color variants so that I can surface contextual messages.
Expected Behaviour: .alert is a flex row, bordered, --radius-lg, text-sm, relative. Direct-child svg sized 1.125rem. Variants destructive/success/warning/info shift border-color and recolor svg+title.
Edge Cases: Variant without an svg child; Multiple svgs; Default variant equals background
Notes: Rules: Icon styling targets only direct-child svg via > | Title recolor scoped to .alert-title || Deps: base/tokens (--destructive,--success,--warning,--info) | base/theme || Assumes: Tokens --success,--warning,--info exist

User Story: As a developer I want a closable alert without JavaScript so that users can dismiss banners.
Expected Behaviour: Using <details open> as the alert, clicking summary removes [open]; .alert-dismissible:not([open]) sets display:none. Padding-inline-end added only when role=alert AND alert-dismissible.
Edge Cases: Dismissal is irreversible; Padding reserve depends on role=alert being present
Notes: Rules: Close affordance is the <summary>; marker hidden | Collapse rule keyed on :not([open]) || Deps: Native <details>/<summary> | CSS attribute selectors || Assumes: Author wraps alert as <details class=alert ... alert-dismissible open>

User Story: As a developer I want a destructive-action confirmation modal so that users confirm before irreversible actions.
Expected Behaviour: dialog.alert-dialog centers, bordered, --radius-lg, --shadow-lg, max-width min(28rem,90vw), enter animation; ::backdrop blurred. Header centered, footer flex with data-justify center/between. Cancel uses <form method=dialog>.
Edge Cases: Opening requires showModal() (JS); Long content vs overflow:hidden
Test Cases: SUR-005-T1:happy:PASS; SUR-005-T2:error:FAIL; SUR-005-T3:boundary:FAIL; SUR-005-T4:responsive:PASS; SUR-005-T5:accessibility:FAIL; SUR-005-T6:happy:PASS
Notes: Rules: data-justify supports center and between; default flex-start | Backdrop blur prefixed for webkit || Deps: Native <dialog> | registry dep on dialog | base/tokens, base/theme || Assumes: Confirm button wired by author; Cancel via method=dialog

User Story: As a developer I want a thin divider in either orientation so that I can visually separate content.
Expected Behaviour: .separator is a flex-shrink:0 border-colored block; horizontal = 100% wide x 1px, vertical = 100% tall x 1px with align-self:stretch; default = horizontal.
Edge Cases: Vertical separator needs a definite parent height; No data-orientation falls back to horizontal
Notes: Rules: data-orientation accepts horizontal|vertical | Default branch via :not([data-orientation]) || Deps: base/tokens (--border) || Assumes: Author sets height for vertical separators in non-stretch contexts

User Story: As a tooling author I want machine-readable component metadata so that I can install components programmatically.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares $schema as shadcn schema then uses a custom shape: top-level name/version/style/tokens and a components array of {name,file,deps,description,markup,classes,attributes}.
Edge Cases: Schema URL implies shadcn registry shape but objects use non-shadcn keys; alert-dialog markup includes role-less, label-less dialog
Notes: Rules: Each entry cites a real file path | classes arrays should match CSS class names || Deps: Cited $schema https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json || Assumes: Consumers may validate against the cited schema

User Story: As a developer I want an avatar component with size/shape/ring/group variants so that I can show user images with a text fallback.
Expected Behaviour: `.avatar` is a 2.5rem inline-flex circle with muted bg, centering text fallback and clipping > img via object-fit:cover. Size variants sm/lg/xl, .avatar-square, .avatar-ring, and overlapping .avatar-group.
Edge Cases: img fails to load -> no CSS fallback, relies on inline onerror JS; very long text fallback overflowing the fixed box; RTL: margin-inline-start used
Test Cases: MED-001-T1:happy:PASS; MED-001-T2:happy:FAIL; MED-001-T3:error:FAIL; MED-001-T4:boundary:PASS; MED-001-T5:accessibility:WAIVED; MED-001-T6:responsive:PASS
Notes: Rules: fallback text centered when no img | img clipped to circle via overflow:hidden + object-fit:cover | group children overlap except first || Deps: base/tokens (--muted,--muted-foreground,--background,--border,--radius-full,--radius-md,--text-*) || Assumes: Consumer supplies text fallback as element text content

User Story: As a developer I want shimmering placeholder blocks so that loading states look intentional without JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.skeleton` is a block with a 3-stop muted gradient animated by shadcss-shimmer 1.5s infinite. .skeleton-circle rounds fully; .skeleton-text last-child 60% width. Animation disabled under reduced-motion.
Edge Cases: last-child override forces 60% width even when author wants full width; reduced-motion users get a static gradient
Notes: Rules: shimmer loops infinitely | reduced motion removes animation | circle/text modifiers adjust radius/height || Deps: base/tokens (--muted,--radius-sm,--radius-full) || Assumes: Placeholder dimensions set by consumer via inline style

User Story: As a developer I want a pure-CSS spinner with sizes and colors so that I can show inline/standalone loading.
Expected Behaviour: `.spinner` is a 1.25rem ring spun by shadcss-spin. Size variants sm/lg/xl; color variants; .spinner-inline. Reduced motion slows to 1.5s rather than stopping.
Edge Cases: reduced-motion still spins (slower); spinner-inherit makes top border transparent
Test Cases: MED-003-T1:happy:PASS; MED-003-T2:happy:PASS; MED-003-T3:accessibility:WAIVED; MED-003-T4:boundary:PASS; MED-003-T5:accessibility:FAIL; MED-003-T6:invalid:PASS
Notes: Rules: all color variants reference defined tokens | spin keyframe rotates 360deg | reduced-motion slows but keeps motion || Deps: base/tokens || Assumes: Consumer adds aria-label/role=status if needed

User Story: As a developer I want aspect-ratio presets so that media stays at a fixed ratio responsively.
Expected Behaviour: `.aspect-ratio` is a full-width relative box defaulting 16/9 with overflow hidden and radius-md; direct children absolutely positioned to fill and cover. Presets square/video/photo/portrait/cinema/wide/ultra.
Edge Cases: height grows from width; in a flex row with no width may collapse; absolute children mean text/non-media content gets stretched & clipped; border-radius:md may clip unexpectedly
Notes: Rules: uses native aspect-ratio property | children cover the box | presets set correct ratios || Deps: base/tokens (--radius-md) | modern CSS aspect-ratio property || Assumes: Consumer constrains width

User Story: As a tooling consumer I want registry.json to describe components per its declared $schema so that automated installers can consume it.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares $schema https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json then lists components as {name,file,deps,markup,classes} objects.
Edge Cases: schema mismatch breaks shadcn CLI consumption; class lists must match actual CSS classes
Notes: Rules: component class lists match CSS | shape conforms to cited schema || Deps: shadcn registry schema || Assumes: The cited schema is the shadcn registry schema

User Story: As a developer I want a styled native dialog modal so that I can show focused content with a backdrop and a no-JS close button.
Expected Behaviour: dialog.dialog styles a native <dialog> with border, lg radius, background, shadow-lg, max-width min(32rem,90vw), max-height 85vh, overflow auto, fade/translate-in animation; ::backdrop blur. Header/body/footer/close subparts.
Edge Cases: Modal open requires showModal() JS; Long content scrolls via overflow:auto + max-height 85vh; dialog-close is position:absolute
Notes: Rules: Backdrop styling only applies when opened via showModal() | ::backdrop animation must be defined | close button needs an accessible name and a click handler || Deps: base/tokens | native <dialog> + showModal() | registry deps: base/tokens, base/theme || Assumes: Author opens via showModal() | Consumer supplies close wiring or uses <form method=dialog>

User Story: As a developer I want a full-height slide-in sheet from any edge so that I can present secondary navigation or content over the page.
Expected Behaviour: dialog.sheet is a borderless full-height panel max-width 32rem with shadow-2xl and blurred backdrop; data-side right/left/top/bottom set insets and a directional slide animation. Header/body/footer subparts.
Edge Cases: top/bottom sides switch to 100vw width and auto height max 90vh; Right is default in showcase via data-side=right; No data-side => no side-specific inset or slide animation
Notes: Rules: Each data-side defines matching keyframes | Opening requires showModal() for backdrop | sheet-footer data-justify accepts only end|between || Deps: base/tokens | native <dialog> + showModal() | registry deps & markup || Assumes: Consumer always supplies a data-side | Opened modally

User Story: As a developer I want a narrow drawer that slides from an edge so that I can show filters or secondary actions.
Expected Behaviour: dialog.drawer is a borderless full-height max-width 24rem panel with shadow-xl and a 2px-blur backdrop; base animation slide-in. data-side left/top/bottom override inset + animation-name; right relies on base animation.
Edge Cases: Right side has no explicit animation-name and inherits base; top/bottom switch to 100vw width, auto height max 80vh; footer only defines data-justify=end
Notes: Rules: Each non-right side sets animation-name | Opening requires showModal() for backdrop || Deps: base/tokens | native <dialog> + showModal() | registry deps & markup || Assumes: Right is the default visual | Opened modally

User Story: As a developer I want a click-triggered floating panel with arbitrary content so that I can show forms or details without JS.
Expected Behaviour: .popover is a 20rem padded panel using --popover tokens, border, lg radius, shadow-md, margin-top spacing and fade/translate-in animation; data-side top/start/end adjust margins; size variants. Triggered via popovertarget.
Edge Cases: Popover API has no native anchor positioning here; inset+margins only nudge it; data-side values are top/start/end (no bottom); popover-sm/lg only change width+padding
Notes: Rules: Trigger must use popovertarget matching the popover id | Real positioning requires CSS anchor positioning or JS | Browser must support Popover API || Deps: Popover API | base/tokens | registry deps base/tokens, popover || Assumes: Consumer accepts top-layer default placement | Modern browser with Popover API

Feature Name: Registry schema conformance for overlay components
User Story: As a tooling author I want the registry to conform to its declared $schema so that shadcn-compatible CLIs can consume it.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares $schema https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json and lists overlay components dialog/drawer/sheet/popover with a custom shape using deps/markup/classes/description fields.
Edge Cases: shadcn registry schema expects items with type registry:* and files[]; No per-item type field present
Notes: Rules: If $schema is shadcn, items must match shadcn registry-item shape | deps/markup/classes are non-standard keys || Deps: shadcn registry JSON schema || Assumes: The cited schema is the authoritative contract

User Story: As a developer I want a click-triggered dropdown menu so that I can show actions without writing open/close JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.dropdown-menu` is a popover styled with min-width 12rem, popover surface tokens, scale-in animation; opened via native popovertarget/popover attrs. Items get hover/focus accent, destructive variant, separators, labels, shortcuts.
Edge Cases: Browsers without Popover API render menu inline/visible; Anchor positioning unsupported: data-side falls back to inset/margin nudges; Long item text vs width:max-content
Notes: Rules: Trigger must use popovertarget matching menu id | Menu element must carry both popover attr and id | data-side limited to bottom-start|bottom-end|top-start|top-end || Deps: base/tokens | Native Popover API | btn component for trigger || Assumes: Showcase loads a browser supporting popover | Tokens layer loaded before components

User Story: As a developer I want a horizontal menubar (File/Edit/View) whose triggers open dropdown panels so that I can build app-style menus.
Expected Behaviour: `.menubar` is an inline-flex bordered bar; .menubar-trigger are buttons; .menubar-menu is a popover reusing dropdown-item styles, fixed to inset bottom-start with margin-top.
Edge Cases: Multiple menus open: native popover auto-mode closes others; menubar-menu has hard-coded bottom-start position only; Reused dropdown-item rules require dropdown.css present
Notes: Rules: menubar-menu must follow its trigger and have popover+id | Requires dropdown.css loaded | Trigger uses popovertarget || Deps: dropdown | base/tokens | Popover API || Assumes: dropdown.css always bundled with menubar | Browser supports popover

Feature Name: Navigation menu with rich link panels
User Story: As a developer I want a horizontal nav whose triggers open rich link panels so that I can build a marketing mega-menu.
Expected Behaviour: `.navigation-menu` z-indexed container; triggers with chevron ::after mask; .navigation-menu-content is absolutely positioned panel with link grid, supporting data-cols=2.
Edge Cases: Content uses position:absolute but showcase also adds popover attr -> top-layer conflict; data-cols only handles 1 or 2; Long descriptions wrap; min-width 20rem fixed
Notes: Rules: Trigger popovertarget must match content id | data-cols in {default(1),2} | navigation-menu-item must be positioned ancestor || Deps: base/tokens | Popover API / absolute positioning | secondary tokens for link icon || Assumes: Browser supports mask for chevron | Popover and absolute positioning coexist

User Story: As a developer I want a right-click context menu so that I can show contextual actions at the cursor.
Expected Behaviour: `.context-menu` is a popover-styled surface reusing dropdown-item styles, with checkbox-style items (data-checked) and submenu arrow indicators; CSS header states it requires Popover API trigger=manual + inline oncontextmenu to position.
Edge Cases: No positioning logic in CSS — cursor placement depends on inline JS the showcase never provides; data-checked toggling needs JS; Submenu arrow is decorative only
Notes: Rules: Requires inline oncontextmenu + showPopover() | Depends on dropdown.css for item styling | context-menu-item-check[data-checked=true] for checked state || Deps: dropdown | base/tokens | Popover API + inline JS || Assumes: Consumer adds the oncontextmenu JS themselves

Feature Name: Registry schema conformance for menu components
User Story: As a tooling author I want registry.json to match its declared $schema so that shadcn-compatible installers can consume these components.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares shadcn $schema but each item uses a custom shape name/file/deps/markup/classes instead of shadcn registry-item fields.
Edge Cases: shadcn validators reject unknown shape; deps vs registryDependencies naming; file (string) vs files (array)
Notes: Rules: Items should have type + files[] per shadcn schema | registryDependencies not deps || Deps: shadcn registry schema || Assumes: The cited $schema is the authoritative shape

User Story: As a developer I want a zero-JS tooltip on hover/focus so that I can annotate triggers without scripting.
Expected Behaviour: `.tooltip::after` renders attr(data-tooltip) hidden and becomes visible on :hover/:focus-within. Four positions via data-position top(default)/bottom/start/end.
Edge Cases: Long text uses white-space:nowrap so it never wraps and can overflow viewport; Empty data-tooltip renders an empty styled box on hover; No collision detection: start/end near edge clips offscreen
Test Cases: DIS-001-T1:happy:PASS; DIS-001-T2:accessibility:FAIL; DIS-001-T3:accessibility:PASS; DIS-001-T4:boundary:FAIL; DIS-001-T5:invalid:PASS; DIS-001-T6:security:PASS
Notes: Rules: data-tooltip attribute must be present for content | data-position limited to bottom/start/end; any other value falls back to top || Deps: base/tokens (--primary,--text-xs,--radius-sm,--shadow-md,--z-overlay,--duration-fast,--ease-out) || Assumes: Trigger element can receive focus for keyboard reveal

User Story: As a developer I want a rich preview card on hover so that I can show profile/stat content without JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.hover-card-panel` is hidden by default and shown via display:block on :hover/:focus-within only under @media (hover:hover) and (pointer:fine). On @media (hover:none) the panel stays display:none. Entry animated via keyframes.
Edge Cases: Touch devices: panel permanently hidden with no tap fallback despite comment claiming one; Default (no media match) leaves panel with no display rule, so it is visible/block by default
Notes: Rules: Requires .hover-card wrapper around trigger + .hover-card-panel sibling || Deps: base/tokens (--popover,--border,--radius-lg,--shadow-md,--space-*) | @media (hover/pointer) feature queries || Assumes: File header claims Popover API usage; markup does not use it

User Story: As a developer I want accessible collapsible panels so that I get keyboard and SR support without JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.accordion > details > summary` styled with custom chevron rotating 180deg when [open]. Content animated via summary~* grid-template-rows 0fr->1fr. Exclusive single-open via native name attribute.
Edge Cases: Animated reveal needs grid 0fr/1fr support; data-chevron=false hides marker; Default details marker hidden
Test Cases: DIS-003-T1:happy:PASS; DIS-003-T2:happy:PASS; DIS-003-T3:boundary:PASS; DIS-003-T4:accessibility:PASS; DIS-003-T5:invalid:PASS; DIS-003-T6:performance:PASS
Notes: Rules: Content must be a sibling element after summary to be animated | Exclusive mode requires matching name on each <details> || Deps: Native <details>/<summary> | base/tokens | grid-template-rows interpolation || Assumes: Authors place content in .accordion-content div

User Story: As a developer I want a minimal collapsible so that I can toggle arbitrary content with a styled trigger.
Expected Behaviour: `.collapsible` is a <details>; .collapsible-trigger is the <summary> with chevron rotating on [open]. Content .collapsible-content animated grid 0fr->1fr when .collapsible[open]. Focus ring on :focus-visible.
Edge Cases: [open] attribute lives on the <details>.collapsible; No border/chevron chrome like accordion by design
Notes: Rules: Trigger must be the summary with class collapsible-trigger | Content must be sibling after trigger with class collapsible-content || Deps: Native <details>/<summary> | base/tokens (--ring,--space-*,--radius-md) || Assumes: Markup matches registry example <details class=collapsible>

User Story: As a tooling author I want registry.json to describe components accurately so that codegen/install works.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares $schema https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json and a custom top-level shape.
Edge Cases: shadcn schema expects different keys; tooltip entry omits the four position values enumeration check
Notes: Rules: If $schema is cited it should validate the document | Each component file path must exist || Deps: shadcn registry JSON schema (cited) || Assumes: Consumers may attempt schema validation

User Story: As a developer I want tab panels that switch with zero JavaScript so that I can use tabs in static HTML.
Expected Behaviour: Hidden radio inputs drive state; checked radio sibling label gets active styling and the matching panel is shown via .tabs:has(input[id$=-N]:checked) .tabs-panel[data-tab$=-N] for suffixes -1..-12. Panels default display:none.
Edge Cases: More than 12 tabs (no pre-baked rule for -13+); Two panels within one .tabs sharing the same numeric suffix; ids whose suffix overlaps
Test Cases: NAV-001-T1:happy:PASS; NAV-001-T2:boundary:FAIL; NAV-001-T3:accessibility:FAIL; NAV-001-T4:invalid:FAIL; NAV-001-T5:boundary:PASS; NAV-001-T6:responsive:PASS
Notes: Rules: Each input id must end in -1..-12 | panel data-tab must end in matching suffix | input and label must be siblings || Deps: :has() selector support | attribute substring | CSS custom property tokens || Assumes: Author follows documented suffix convention

User Story: As a developer I want a hierarchical nav trail with separators so users see their location.
Expected Behaviour: Flex wrap container; each item except last gets a / separator via ::after; links hover to foreground/accent; current item bold foreground; custom separators via data-separator.
Edge Cases: Single item (no separator); data-separator value other than > or backslash; Very long trail wrapping
Notes: Rules: Last item must not render separator | breadcrumb-current marks active page || Deps: ::after pseudo-element | tokens (muted-foreground, accent) || Assumes: Author uses span.breadcrumb-item wrappers

User Story: As a developer I want page navigation links with active and disabled states.
Expected Behaviour: Flex list; links 2rem square; active page via aria-current=page gets primary bg and pointer-events:none; prev/next disabled via aria-disabled=true dims and disables; ellipsis styled span.
Edge Cases: aria-disabled prev still focusable via keyboard; active link still in tab order despite pointer-events:none; ellipsis is non-interactive
Notes: Rules: Active page must carry aria-current=page | Disabled prev/next must carry aria-disabled=true || Deps: tokens (primary, accent, ring) | attribute selectors || Assumes: Author uses <a> elements

User Story: As a developer I want an app sidebar with header/content/footer and collapse/mobile behavior.
Expected Behaviour: Fixed-width 16rem sticky column; collapsed width 4rem gated on data-collapsible=icon AND data-collapsed=true; active item via aria-current=page or data-active=true; mobile <768px becomes off-canvas drawer toggled by data-mobile-open=true; inset/floating variants.
Edge Cases: Collapse requires JS to toggle data-collapsed (none provided); Mobile drawer requires JS to set data-mobile-open / data-mobile; Backdrop ::before only renders when data-mobile=true
Test Cases: NAV-004-T1:happy:PASS; NAV-004-T2:happy:PASS; NAV-004-T3:error:FAIL; NAV-004-T4:responsive:FAIL; NAV-004-T5:boundary:PASS; NAV-004-T6:accessibility:FAIL
Notes: Rules: Collapse needs BOTH data-collapsible=icon and data-collapsed=true | Mobile open needs data-mobile-open=true || Deps: sidebar-* tokens | --z-overlay token | JS to mutate data-collapsed / data-mobile-open (not shipped) || Assumes: Routing/state layer toggles attributes

User Story: As a tooling author I want registry.json to describe components per its declared schema.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares $schema https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json but top-level shape uses name/version/components[] with per-item file/deps/markup/classes which does not conform to shadcn schema.
Edge Cases: Validators fetching the cited $schema will reject the document; Components (50) label vs README 50+ claim
Notes: Rules: If $schema is cited, document should validate against it || Deps: shadcn registry JSON schema || Assumes: Schema URL is intended as authoritative

User Story: As a developer I want to drop standard <table> markup with a class so that I get a styled, scrollable, hover-aware data table without JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.table-wrapper` provides overflow-x scroll + border; .table collapses borders, sm font; thead muted bg; rows hover-tint and last-row border removed; data-align attrs set text-align; compact + sticky-header variants.
Edge Cases: Wide tables overflowing viewport; Sticky header inside non-scrolling ancestor; data-align with invalid value
Notes: Rules: data-align must be end or center to take effect | Sticky header requires scrollable wrapper || Deps: base/tokens || Assumes: Consumer supplies semantic <thead>/<tbody>

Feature Name: Calendar — pure-CSS month grid (no navigation logic)
User Story: As a developer I want a styled month calendar with today/selected/disabled/range states so that I can present dates, wiring real date behavior myself.
Expected Behaviour: 7-col grid; day cells with hover/focus-visible; selected via [aria-pressed=true] and today via .is-today share primary bg; today-not-selected gets ring border; disabled dims + pointer-events:none; range start/middle/end radii; .calendar-sm compact.
Edge Cases: Both is-today and aria-pressed on same cell; Range without middle cells; Keyboard navigation across grid; Month switching
Test Cases: DAT-002-T1:happy:PASS; DAT-002-T2:error:FAIL; DAT-002-T3:boundary:PASS; DAT-002-T4:accessibility:FAIL; DAT-002-T5:invalid:PASS; DAT-002-T6:responsive:PASS
Notes: Rules: Selected state keyed on aria-pressed=true | Disabled keyed on aria-disabled=true || Deps: base/tokens | Consumer JS for navigation/date logic || Assumes: Consumer sets aria-pressed/aria-disabled/is-today correctly

User Story: As a developer I want a touch- and keyboard-friendly scroll-snap carousel with size presets, arrows, and dots so that I can show horizontally scrollable content.
Expected Behaviour: Viewport scrolls x with mandatory snap + hidden scrollbar + smooth behavior; flex track; items snap-align:start, full width default with half/third/quarter presets; presets collapse to 80% under 640px; arrows and dots styled controls.
Edge Cases: Reduced-motion users and scroll-behavior:smooth; Dots reflecting active slide; Arrow buttons advancing without JS
Test Cases: DAT-003-T1:happy:PASS; DAT-003-T2:error:FAIL; DAT-003-T3:error:FAIL; DAT-003-T4:boundary:PASS; DAT-003-T5:responsive:PASS; DAT-003-T6:accessibility:FAIL
Notes: Rules: data-size must be half/third/quarter to alter width || Deps: base/tokens | Inline JS for arrow navigation | Native scroll-snap || Assumes: Scrolling itself works via native overflow

Feature Name: Command palette — styled shell (no search/nav logic)
User Story: As a developer I want a cmdk-styled command palette shell I can open from a Cmd-K trigger via the Popover API, wiring fuzzy search and arrow-nav myself.
Expected Behaviour: `.command` is a clamped popover-styled flex column; input row; scrollable list with custom scrollbar; groups/headings/items/separator/shortcut/empty/footer styling; selected item keyed on [data-selected=true].
Edge Cases: Open via Popover API; Keyboard arrow navigation between items; Empty/no-results state; Long list scrolling
Notes: Rules: Active item keyed on data-selected=true | Intended for use with popover attribute + popovertarget trigger || Deps: base/tokens | Popover API | Consumer JS for fuzzy search + arrow-key nav || Assumes: Consumer adds popover attribute and trigger

User Story: As a tooling/CLI consumer I want registry.json to describe each component accurately against its declared schema so that automated installs and docs are trustworthy.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares shadcn $schema and lists per-component name/file/deps/description/markup/classes; command deps include popover; table classes limited to wrapper/table/compact.
Edge Cases: Schema conformance vs cited shadcn schema; Class list completeness vs CSS; Deps accuracy
Notes: Rules: $schema cited is https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json | classes arrays should reflect actual selectors || Deps: shadcn registry schema (cited but not locally validated) || Assumes: Consumers may validate against the cited schema

User Story: As a developer I want a CSS-styled toast so that I can show transient notifications that auto-dismiss without writing JS.
Expected Behaviour: `.toast` renders a card with in/out animations; the out animation is delayed by --toast-duration (5s) and runs forwards to hide. [data-persistent=true] disables auto-dismiss. Variants color svg/title and border.
Edge Cases: data-persistent toast never dismisses; prefers-reduced-motion disables animation, leaving toast visible; top-position uses translateY animations
Test Cases: FBK-001-T1:happy:PASS; FBK-001-T2:error:FAIL; FBK-001-T3:boundary:PASS; FBK-001-T4:accessibility:FAIL; FBK-001-T5:accessibility:PASS; FBK-001-T6:invalid:FAIL
Notes: Rules: Out animation only hides opacity/transform, element still occupies layout | Auto-dismiss depends on element being rendered on mount || Deps: base/tokens | Popover API | CSS animations || Assumes: Registry markup uses popover attribute + onclick hidePopover

User Story: As a developer I want a richer toast with action button and loading spinner so that I can mirror shadcn Sonner.
Expected Behaviour: `.sonner` card with in/out animations delayed by --sonner-duration (4s) forwards; out animation collapses height/padding/margin/border for stack reflow. .sonner-loading > svg spins. Position variants reposition region.
Edge Cases: loading variant relies on shadcss-spin keyframe defined elsewhere; out animation collapses box for smooth stack; data-persistent disables dismiss
Test Cases: FBK-002-T1:happy:PASS; FBK-002-T2:error:FAIL; FBK-002-T3:error:FAIL; FBK-002-T4:boundary:PASS; FBK-002-T5:responsive:PASS; FBK-002-T6:accessibility:FAIL; FBK-002-T7:invalid:FAIL
Notes: Rules: shadcss-spin must exist in another component file | region width clamps to viewport || Deps: base/tokens | .btn (sonner-action) | shadcss-spin keyframe (external) | Popover API || Assumes: Showcase applies popover to .sonner directly

User Story: As a developer I want a centered empty-state block so that I can show friendly placeholders with icon, title, description, and CTAs.
Expected Behaviour: `.empty` centers content column with gap/padding; .empty-icon is a 3rem rounded accent circle; description clamps to 32rem; error/success/warning variants recolor icon.
Edge Cases: no icon: layout still centers; long description wraps within 32rem; actions row optional
Notes: Rules: variants only restyle .empty-icon | pure presentational, no JS || Deps: base/tokens | .btn for actions || Assumes: registry markup matches classes

User Story: As a developer I want a thin custom scrollbar so that scrollable regions match the design system.
Expected Behaviour: `.scroll-area` sets overflow:auto + Firefox scrollbar-width:thin/color, and WebKit pseudo-element scrollbar styling. Size variants -sm/-lg adjust WebKit width. -hidden removes scrollbar. data-persistent recolors track.
Edge Cases: Firefox ignores ::-webkit pseudo; uses scrollbar-color only; -sm/-lg only affect WebKit, not Firefox thickness; -ms-overflow-style targets legacy Edge/IE
Notes: Rules: scrollbar-width:thin not adjustable per-variant in Firefox | persistent only changes color || Deps: base/tokens (--border,--input,--muted) || Assumes: used on sidebar

User Story: As a docs author I want to show keyboard shortcuts so that users can see key combinations rendered as physical key caps.
Expected Behaviour: Both kbd element and .kbd class render as inline-flex centered cap with min-width 1.5rem, height 1.25rem, mono font, muted bg, border with 2px bottom border for a key look.
Edge Cases: Multi-char content like esc exceeding min-width relies on white-space:nowrap; Used inside .prose where typography.css redefines kbd
Notes: Rules: Requires tokens --font-mono, --text-xs, --foreground, --muted, --border, --radius-sm, --shadow-xs || Deps: base/tokens || Assumes: --shadow-xs token defined

User Story: As a page author I want a centered max-width wrapper so that content does not stretch full-bleed on large screens.
Expected Behaviour: .container is full-width, auto inline margins, inline padding --space-4 (->--space-6 at >=640px), max-width 80rem; size modifiers set 40/56/72/80rem and full=100%.
Edge Cases: Modifier order: .container-sm after .container both in same layer; container-full=100% removes cap
Notes: Rules: Requires --space-4, --space-6 tokens || Deps: base/tokens || Assumes: Registry lists container

User Story: As a developer I want a panel group with divider affordance so that I can attach ~10 lines of JS for actual drag-resize.
Expected Behaviour: Grid layout switches axis via data-direction; panels get card bg/border and rounded outer corners; handles show grip ::after, axis-specific cursor, hover/active ring color, and focus-visible outline. Pure CSS cannot drag — explicitly acknowledged.
Edge Cases: Vertical direction panels keep horizontal-only border-radius; Equal sizing via 1fr auto-columns; Handle relies on JS to set data-active
Test Cases: MSC-003-T1:happy:PASS; MSC-003-T2:accessibility:FAIL; MSC-003-T3:boundary:FAIL; MSC-003-T4:performance:PASS; MSC-003-T5:error:WAIVED; MSC-003-T6:invalid:FAIL
Notes: Rules: Requires data-direction attribute; tokens --card,--border,--ring,--muted-foreground,--radius-* || Deps: base/tokens | JS for drag | data-direction attribute || Assumes: handle has explicit width 4px

User Story: As a content author I want drop-in prose styling so that long-form HTML looks polished without per-element classes.
Expected Behaviour: .prose sets base color/size/line-height and 70ch measure; descendant selectors style h1-h4, p, a, lists, blockquote, code/pre, hr, img, table, kbd, plus .lead and mark helpers; vertical rhythm via > * + *.
Edge Cases: Adjacent-sibling margin plus per-element margins can double-stack; mark uses --warning token at 0.3 alpha; Demo uses prose-demo override max-width 65ch
Test Cases: MSC-004-T1:happy:PASS; MSC-004-T2:boundary:PASS; MSC-004-T3:accessibility:PASS; MSC-004-T4:error:PASS; MSC-004-T5:responsive:PASS; MSC-004-T6:invalid:PASS
Notes: Rules: Requires text/space/radius/color tokens and --font-mono, --warning || Deps: base/tokens | --warning token || Assumes: --text-lg/2xl/3xl/xl defined

User Story: As an AI agent I want registry.json to be machine-readable and schema-valid so that I can generate correct markup.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares shadcn $schema but uses a custom shape not matching shadcn registry item schema; describes these four components.
Edge Cases: $schema URL implies shadcn validation that the document fails; Footer claims 0 lines of JS while resizable requires JS
Notes: Rules: Items should match cited $schema || Deps: shadcn schema || Assumes: Consumers may validate against the cited schema

User Story: As a developer I want a complete set of HSL-based design tokens so that I can build and theme components consistently.
Expected Behaviour: :root defines 12 token groups as custom properties plus breakpoints. Tokens are unlayered (no @layer) so they apply at all cascade levels.
Edge Cases: Token referenced by a component but absent from a theme block; calc()-derived radii when --radius is overridden; HSL triplet consumed without hsl() wrapper
Notes: Rules: Every token used must be defined in :root | Color tokens are space-separated HSL triplets | Derived tokens must reference --radius via calc() || Deps: theme.css and component files consume these || Assumes: Consumers wrap color tokens in hsl(var(--x))

User Story: As a user I want the UI to follow my OS theme or an explicit toggle so that I get a consistent light or dark appearance.
Expected Behaviour: Light is the :root default with color-scheme:light. Explicit dark via [data-theme=dark]. Auto dark via @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) on :root:not([data-theme=light]).
Edge Cases: OS dark + data-theme=light (force light); OS dark + no data-theme (auto dark); OS light + data-theme=dark (force dark); Token present in explicit dark but absent in auto-dark block
Notes: Rules: data-theme=light must override OS dark | Explicit and auto dark blocks must define the same token set | color-scheme must flip with theme || Deps: body bg/color in theme.css consume --background/--foreground || Assumes: A toggle sets data-theme on a root element

User Story: As a developer I want a predictable layer order so component styles win over reset/theme without !important.
Expected Behaviour: Three named layers: reset, theme, components. No explicit @layer priming statement exists; order is established by first appearance. shadcss.css imports reset->tokens->theme->components and dist concatenates in the same order.
Edge Cases: A bundler reorders @imports; A consumer imports a component file before reset; Unlayered token rules vs layered rules
Notes: Rules: First-appearance order must be reset < theme < components | Tokens (unlayered) must override layered defaults | An explicit @layer priming statement would harden order || Deps: @import order in shadcss.css; concatenation order in dist build || Assumes: Browser supports @layer

User Story: As a developer I want a normalized baseline so components render consistently across browsers without margin/box-sizing surprises.
Expected Behaviour: Reset layer sets border-box, zeroes margin/padding, normalizes html, body, headings text-wrap, list role opt-out, media block display, form control inheritance, button cursor, anchor inherit, dialog/popover :where() normalization at zero specificity.
Edge Cases: Author wants list bullets back; Native dialog default centering overridden; button without explicit type inside form
Notes: Rules: Reset must live in lowest layer | List-style only removed when role=list | Form controls inherit font/color || Deps: @layer reset must be first-appearance || Assumes: Target browsers support text-wrap:balance/pretty and :popover-open

User Story: As a motion-sensitive user I want animations suppressed when I request reduced motion so the UI does not trigger discomfort.
Expected Behaviour: A global @media(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block forces animation/transition-duration to 0.01ms, iteration-count 1, scroll-behavior auto, all with !important, placed OUTSIDE @layer reset so its !important wins globally.
Edge Cases: Component sets animation via !important; Infinite spinner animation; User toggles OS setting at runtime
Notes: Rules: Motion overrides must be unbeatable by component CSS | Must cap iteration-count to stop infinite loops | Should not break essential transitions (uses 0.01ms not 0) || Deps: None — self-contained media query || Assumes: Components rely on transition/animation, not JS-driven motion

User Story: As a developer I want one entrypoint that pulls base + components in correct order so I can opt out of components by deleting imports.
Expected Behaviour: shadcss.css is purely @import statements: base reset/tokens/theme first, then 52 component imports. No CSS rules precede the imports. dist/shadcss.css is the pre-concatenated bundle.
Edge Cases: Removing a component import to drop it; Bundler that does not resolve relative @import; Missing target file
Notes: Rules: All @import must precede any style rule | Each import path must resolve to an existing file | Order: reset, tokens, theme, then components || Deps: All referenced base + component files must exist || Assumes: A build step produces dist from these imports

User Story: As a maintainer I want npm run build to bundle src/shadcss.css and emit expanded + minified dist files so consumers get a ready-to-use stylesheet.
Expected Behaviour: build() ensures dist/, bundles src entry via lightningcss bundle() with browser targets, writes dist/shadcss.css, minifies via transform(), writes dist/shadcss.min.css, prints sizes. bundle reads from disk by filename.
Edge Cases: src entry missing -> readFile rejects, caught and exit 1; watch mode debounces 100ms; @import resolution handled by bundle()
Notes: Rules: dist/shadcss.css and dist/shadcss.min.css must be written | targets pin chrome111/firefox113/safari16/edge111 || Deps: lightningcss ^1.29.0 | node:fs, node:zlib, node:url || Assumes: src/shadcss.css exists | lightningcss native binary present

User Story: As a contributor I want root scripts to build/dev/publish the shadcss package and run the www showcase across the monorepo.
Expected Behaviour: Root build/dev delegate to packages/shadcss; www delegates to apps/www; release builds then npm publish --workspace=packages/shadcss. Workspaces=apps/*,packages/*.
Edge Cases: release runs npm publish but packageManager is pnpm; www dev uses npx serve; www build is a no-op echo
Notes: Rules: build must target packages/shadcss | release must build before publish || Deps: npm workspaces; apps/www and packages/shadcss || Assumes: Contributors use npm despite packageManager=pnpm

User Story: As a developer I want one declared package manager and matching lockfile so installs are reproducible.
Expected Behaviour: Root declares npm workspaces AND ships pnpm-workspace.yaml with identical globs; packageManager=pnpm@9.0.0; but committed lockfile is package-lock.json (npm).
Edge Cases: Two competing workspace declarations; Lockfile (npm) disagrees with declared pnpm
Notes: Rules: Single source of truth for package manager | Lockfile matches packageManager || Deps: Corepack / package manager resolution || Assumes: CI honors packageManager via corepack

User Story: As a consumer I want npm i shadcss and import shadcss (CSS) to resolve to the built bundle.
Expected Behaviour: package.json exposes main/style=dist/shadcss.min.css, exports ./unminified and ./src, files=[dist,src]. build emits both dist files.
Edge Cases: dist must exist at publish time; package not yet on npm registry
Notes: Rules: Every exports target must exist on disk | files array must include published artifacts || Deps: release script runs build before publish || Assumes: dist committed or built in release flow | npm publish pending

User Story: As a reader I want version numbers and component counts to agree across package.json, README, and source.
Expected Behaviour: Root and package versions 0.1.0; www 0.0.1. README/badge claim 50 components; src has 52 component files / 52 imports.
Edge Cases: Component count drift between marketing copy and source
Notes: Rules: Stated component count must equal actual component files || Deps: src/components/*.css; src/shadcss.css imports || Assumes: Each component = one css file = one import

User Story: As a visitor I want the showcase to load the framework CSS so that components render styled.
Expected Behaviour: index.html loads the framework via relative path ../../packages/shadcss/dist/shadcss.min.css; from apps/www this resolves to packages/shadcss/dist/shadcss.min.css.
Edge Cases: File served from a different root keeps relative path valid; Renamed/missing dist breaks all styling
Notes: Rules: The href target file must exist relative to index.html || Deps: packages/shadcss/dist/shadcss.min.css || Assumes: Dev server roots at apps/www per package.json dev script

User Story: As a developer I want a dev command so that I can preview the showcase locally.
Expected Behaviour: npm run dev runs npx -y serve . -l 3333 serving apps/www on port 3333; build is a no-op echo.
Edge Cases: Port 3333 already in use; No network for npx to fetch serve
Notes: Rules: dev script must launch a static server over index.html || Deps: npx | serve package (fetched on demand) || Assumes: Network available for npx -y

User Story: As a visitor I want the 0 lines of JS claim to be true so that I can trust the framework.
Expected Behaviour: Hero/footer advertise 0 Lines of JS, yet the page wires interactions through inline JS handlers.
Edge Cases: Disabling JS disables dialogs, toasts, theme toggle, slider fill, avatar fallback
Notes: Rules: A 0 JS page must contain no script/inline event handlers

User Story: As a visitor I want to toggle light/dark so that I can view either theme.
Expected Behaviour: Footer button flips document.documentElement.dataset.theme between dark and light via inline onclick; html starts data-theme=light.
Edge Cases: JS disabled: no toggle; Preference not persisted across reloads
Notes: Rules: Toggling must swap the data-theme attribute the CSS keys off || Deps: Theme rules keyed on [data-theme] || Assumes: CSS defines dark variables under [data-theme=dark]

User Story: As a visitor I want to open modal overlays so that I can see dialog components.
Expected Behaviour: Trigger buttons call element.showModal() inline; close uses form method=dialog.
Edge Cases: JS disabled: dialogs never open; ESC closes native dialog for free
Notes: Rules: showModal target IDs must match dialog element IDs || Deps: Native <dialog> | showModal handlers

User Story: As a visitor I want toasts to appear and auto-dismiss so that I see notification behaviour.
Expected Behaviour: Buttons call showPopover(); copy claims auto-dismiss after --sonner-duration then hides itself; close buttons call hidePopover().
Edge Cases: Multiple toasts share identical fixed position — they stack on top of each other; data-persistent toast should not auto-hide
Notes: Rules: Auto-dismiss requires the CSS to actually hide the popover after duration || Deps: Popover API | CSS animation on .sonner || Assumes: shadcss.min.css animates sonner visibility

User Story: As a visitor I want the advertised component count to match what renders so claims are honest.
Expected Behaviour: Page advertises 50+ components and 50 components; sidebar group label says Components (50) but lists only ~18 anchor links.
Edge Cases: Count differs between meta description, hero badge, sidebar, footer
Notes: Rules: A single source of truth for component count

User Story: As a visitor I want the Registry and AI Guide links to open real files so I can use them.
Expected Behaviour: Sidebar links to ./AI_GUIDE.md and ./registry.json relative to apps/www/index.html.
Edge Cases: Files live elsewhere in the repo
Notes: Rules: Relative href targets must exist next to index.html || Deps: apps/www/registry.json | apps/www/AI_GUIDE.md

User Story: As a visitor I want a broken avatar image to fall back to initials so the UI stays clean.
Expected Behaviour: Avatar img uses onerror to hide itself and set parent textContent to U.
Edge Cases: External pravatar.cc unreachable (offline); onerror requires JS
Notes: Rules: Fallback must trigger on image load failure || Deps: Inline onerror JS | external i.pravatar.cc

User Story: As a mobile visitor I want the layout to adapt so content is usable on small screens.
Expected Behaviour: Grid collapses to single column and hides sidebar at <=1024px; sidebar becomes fixed at <=768px; hero uses clamp() font sizing.
Edge Cases: Sidebar hidden but no mobile menu button to reveal it
Notes: Rules: Below breakpoint the main content remains reachable

User Story: As an assistive-tech user I want tabs, menus and tooltips to expose roles so I can operate them.
Expected Behaviour: Tabs use radio inputs + :has(); dropdown/menubar items are links/buttons in popovers; tooltips use data-tooltip attribute rendered via CSS.
Edge Cases: Screen reader announces radios as a radiogroup, not tabs; Tooltip text not in accessibility tree
Notes: Rules: Interactive widgets should carry appropriate ARIA roles

Feature Name: Styled-shell components needing JS (command, carousel, calendar, OTP)
User Story: As a visitor I want interactive demos to actually work, not just look right.
Expected Behaviour: Command palette copy admits it needs ~10 lines of JS; carousel buttons use inline scrollBy JS; calendar days/dots are static buttons; OTP inputs have no auto-advance.
Edge Cases: Calendar prev/next buttons do nothing; Carousel dots are non-functional
Notes: Rules: A demo presented as interactive should perform its core interaction

User Story: As an AI agent or tool consuming the registry, I want registry.json to conform to the shadcn schema it declares so that schema-validating tooling can parse it.
Expected Behaviour: registry.json declares $schema https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json and is structured with top-level name/version/style/tokens/components and per-component name/file/deps/description/markup/classes/attributes.
Edge Cases: Schema URL points to shadcn registry schema but file is a custom shape; Per-item objects omit shadcn-required type field; registryDependencies vs custom deps naming
Notes: Rules: Each registry item must have shadcn-required type | File payloads must be under files as objects with path/type | Cross-component refs must use registryDependencies || Deps: shadcn registry schema || Assumes: The cited schema is the standard shadcn registry schema

Feature Name: registry.json wired to a CLI / consumer
User Story: As a developer, I want a CLI (like shadcn add) to consume registry.json so I can install components, as implied by citing shadcn schema.
Expected Behaviour: No CLI, build script, or app code reads registry.json; it is referenced only in prose docs and the showcase HTML. package.json defines only build/dev scripts.
Edge Cases: Schema citation implies shadcn CLI compatibility that does not exist; registry.json could be consumed at build time but isn't
Notes: Rules: Some script or tool must read registry.json for it to be wired || Deps: package.json scripts | scripts/build.mjs || Assumes: grep across repo is exhaustive for consumers

User Story: As a user evaluating shadcss, I want the zero JavaScript claim to be literally true so I can trust the marketing.
Expected Behaviour: README and root README state 0 lines of JavaScript; the framework CSS ships no JS. AI_GUIDE and registry acknowledge JS one-liners are needed for dialog/popover open and several components.
Edge Cases: Framework CSS vs showcase demo; Inline onclick/showModal counts as JavaScript; Components needing JS
Notes: Rules: No JavaScript anywhere if claim is literal | If JS needed, claim must be qualified at the headline || Deps: apps/www/index.html | AI_GUIDE.md || Assumes: 0 lines of JavaScript as a headline refers to using the library

User Story: As a user, I want the advertised component count to match what ships so the inventory is trustworthy.
Expected Behaviour: README, badges, root README, and AI_GUIDE all state 50 components; registry.json lists 52 items and src/components has 52 .css files.
Edge Cases: radio and radio-group both counted; chart/data-table listed as composed, not files; Comparison table says 50+
Notes: Rules: Claimed count == registry count == file count || Deps: registry.json | src/components/*.css || Assumes: Each registry item maps to one shipped component

User Story: As a user, I want the size claims to match the built artifacts.
Expected Behaviour: README claims ~15.2 KB gzipped, badge 15.2 KB, root badge 15.3 KB; expanded ~152 KB, minified ~127 KB.
Edge Cases: gzip level variance; minified actual 130KB vs claimed 127KB; two READMEs cite 15.2 vs 15.3
Notes: Rules: gzip(dist/shadcss.min.css) ~= claimed 15.x KB | raw/min sizes within stated approximations || Deps: dist/shadcss.min.css | dist/shadcss.css || Assumes: Standard gzip -c approximates over-the-wire size

Feature Name: Install instructions: npm install shadcss / unpkg CDN
User Story: As a user, I want npm install shadcss and the unpkg URL to actually work so I can install the library.
Expected Behaviour: README npm install shadcss, unpkg URL; package.json name is shadcss v0.1.0 with main/style/exports pointing at dist.
Edge Cases: Package not published to npm; unpkg resolves only if published; @import shadcss resolution
Notes: Rules: npm registry must have a shadcss package | unpkg URL must 200 || Deps: npm registry | unpkg CDN || Assumes: registry.npmjs.org/shadcss is the authoritative existence check | npm publish pending

User Story: As a user comparing frameworks, I want the comparison table to be accurate and not misrepresent shadcss or competitors.
Expected Behaviour: README comparison table marks shadcss JS=none, 15.2 KB gzipped, no React, copy-paste yes, 50+ components; competitor rows give approximate sizes/counts.
Edge Cases: shadcss JS=none conflicts with required JS one-liners; 50+ here vs 50 headline vs 52 actual; Bulma/Bootstrap size figures unverified
Notes: Rules: Self-row must reflect true JS requirement | Component count consistent with rest of docs || Deps: README.md comparison table || Assumes: Competitor figures out of scope; focus on shadcss self-claims

User Story: As shadcn/ui author or a cautious user, I want clear attribution and no implication of official endorsement.
Expected Behaviour: Docs describe shadcss as a clone of the shadcn/ui aesthetic, repo mirrors shadcn-ui/ui layout, cites shadcn schema URL, uses shadcn keyword, ships MIT LICENSE.
Edge Cases: Using shadcn name/schema may imply endorsement; No NOTICE/attribution to original shadcn copyright; name shadcss is confusingly similar to shadcn
Notes: Rules: Derivative branding should disclaim official affiliation | If borrowing schema/identity, attribute source || Deps: LICENSE files | README || Assumes: This is an independent project, not affiliated with shadcn
